questions.question: Explain the Liang-Barsky line clipping algorithm. Derive the parametric inequalities used for clipping and state its advantages over the Cohen-Sutherland algorithm.
keyPoints: Parametric form: P(t) = P1 + t(P2 - P1) where 0 ≤ t ≤ 1
Four inequalities: p_i * r + q_i * 0 for i = 1,2,3,4 (p and q derived from edge normals)
Update rules: r1 = max(0, r0, -p/q) for p<0, r2 = min(1, r2, -p/q) for p>0
Advantage: More efficient as it computes intersection points directly without subdivision

questions.question: Using the Sutherland-Hodgman algorithm, clip the polygon with vertices (10,30), (50,10), (80,40), (70,80), (30,70) against the clipping window from (20,20) to (60,60). Show the output vertices after clipping against each edge.
keyPoints: Left edge (x=20): Keep (30,70), (70,80) - intersect (50,10)-(80,40) at x=20 gives y=17.5, intersect (70,80)-(10,30) at x=20 gives y=50
Bottom edge (y=20): From previous, new points - check all vertices against y=20, add intersection points
Right edge (x=60): Clip against x=60, compute intersections using parametric equations
Top edge (y=60): Final clipping, verify all vertices within window bounds

questions.question: (a) Explain the Weiler-Atherton polygon clipping algorithm. How does it handle concave polygons and what data structures are used? (b) Compare Sutherland-Hodgman and Weiler-Atherton algorithms with respect to handling concave polygons and output quality.
keyPoints: WA uses linked lists of vertices, identifies entering and exiting intersection points, traverses polygon boundary
Sutherland-Hodgman clips sequentially against each edge; WA finds all intersections first then reconstructs output
Sutherland-Hodgman produces additional edges for concave polygons; WA handles concave correctly by following polygon boundaries
WA maintains topological correctness; complexity O(N+M) where N=polygon vertices, M=intersection points

questions.question: Write the complete pseudocode for the Liang-Barsky line clipping algorithm. Apply the algorithm to clip the line segment from P1(10, 20) to P2(80, 70) against the rectangular window with corners (30, 10) and (70, 60). Show all calculations step-by-step.
keyPoints: Pseudocode: Initialize p=(dx,-dx,dy,-dy), q=(x1-xmin,xmax-x1,y1-ymin,ymax-y1), r=(0,1), iterate for each edge
Continue for all edges: final r1=0.2857, r2=0.8, clipped points: (30, 34.29) to (60, 60)
